appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of total knee arthroplasty (CPT 27447) for our patient with unilateral primary osteoarthritis of the right knee (ICD-10 M17.11). The denial cited absent supervised PT and pharmacologic management. Updated documentation establishes the full Cigna/EviCore CMM-311 (V2.0.2025) conservative-care criterion: (1) standing AP knee radiograph 2026-03-12 documents Kellgren-Lawrence grade 4 medial-compartment OA with bone-on-bone joint space loss; (2) supervised physical therapy 16 visits across 14 weeks 2025-11-04 through 2026-02-18 with attendance log and weekly progress notes; (3) pharmacologic ladder: acetaminophen 1 g QID inadequate, naproxen 500 mg BID 12 weeks (limited by epigastric pain), duloxetine 30 mg daily 8 weeks for chronic pain; (4) two intra-articular corticosteroid injections 2025-09-22 and 2025-12-15 with documented diminishing duration of relief; (5) WOMAC 78 (severe), Knee Society Score 38, marked functional limitation including inability to descend stairs and reduced 6MWT distance; (6) BMI 31 with active medically-supervised weight management. Per AAOS Clinical Practice Guideline for the Management of Osteoarthritis of the Knee (Non-Arthroplasty), strong recommendations for PT, weight loss, oral and topical NSAIDs, and intra-articular corticosteroids have been exhausted. The patient meets all five CMM-311 criteria. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: imaging, PT log, medication record, injection log, WOMAC/KSS, weight-management documentation.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy / EviCore CMM-311: Knee Replacement Arthroplasty (V2.0.2025, Eff. March 7, 2026)
AAOS Clinical Practice Guideline: Management of Osteoarthritis of the Knee (Non-Arthroplasty), 3rd Edition (2021)
WOMAC Osteoarthritis Index — Validated Outcome Measure
Knee Society Score (KSS) — Validated Outcome Measure

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of arthroscopic medial meniscectomy (CPT 29881) for our patient with a discrete radial tear of the lateral meniscus, right knee (ICD-10 corrected to M23.241, peripheral derangement of lateral meniscus due to old tear). The original denial referenced degenerative meniscal fraying and OA-predominance. Updated documentation: (1) MRI 2026-04-10 reread by musculoskeletal radiologist reports a 9 mm radial tear in the posterior horn of the lateral meniscus extending to the free edge, with associated subchondral edema, and Kellgren-Lawrence grade 1 (mild) OA on standing radiograph — this is NOT an OA-predominant case; (2) mechanical symptoms present: documented true locking episodes 3 times in past 6 weeks with positive McMurray and Apley grind tests; (3) failed conservative management 7 weeks (PT log, NSAID trial, activity modification); (4) symptom-imaging concordance. Per Cigna/EviCore CMM-312 (Eff. March 7, 2026), all four criteria for arthroscopic meniscectomy are satisfied: mechanical symptoms, MRI-confirmed tear unlikely to heal (radial pattern in white-white zone), failed conservative management, and symptoms NOT attributable to OA. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: MRI rereading report, exam documentation, PT log, KL grading.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy / EviCore CMM-312: Knee Surgery — Arthroscopic and Open Procedures (Eff. March 7, 2026)
AAOS Clinical Practice Guideline: Management of Osteoarthritis of the Knee (Non-Arthroplasty), 3rd Edition (2021)

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of single-level posterior lumbar arthrodesis (CPT 22612) at L4-L5 for our patient with degenerative spondylolisthesis grade I and accompanying disc degeneration (ICD-10 M43.16 and M51.36). Updated documentation: (1) flexion-extension lumbar radiographs 2026-04-05 demonstrate 8 mm anterolisthesis of L4 on L5 with 4 mm dynamic translation (mobile spondylolisthesis); (2) MRI 2026-04-08 shows lateral recess stenosis with L5 root impingement matching neurogenic claudication symptoms; (3) supervised PT 18 visits over 16 weeks 2025-11-12 through 2026-03-04 with attendance log; (4) two transforaminal epidural steroid injections 2025-12-19 and 2026-02-12 with transient relief (≤2 weeks each); (5) gabapentin 1800 mg/d × 10 weeks, no meaningful relief; (6) Oswestry Disability Index = 64 (crippled); (7) FABQ-physical = 22 (within reassuring range); (8) neurogenic claudication limiting walking to <100 meters. Per Cigna/EviCore CMM-609 V2.0.2025 and Cigna Medical Coverage Policy 0303, fusion for spondylolisthesis with neural compression after exhausted conservative care meets medical-necessity criteria. NASS Coverage Policy Recommendations for Lumbar Fusion support fusion in this exact scenario. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: flexion-extension films, MRI, PT log, ESI records, medication record, ODI, FABQ.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy / EviCore CMM-609: Lumbar Fusion (Arthrodesis) (V2.0.2025, Eff. December 18, 2025)
Cigna Medical Coverage Policy 0303: Lumbar Fusion for Spinal Instability and Degenerative Disc Conditions
NASS Coverage Recommendations: Lumbar Fusion (2021 update)
Oswestry Disability Index (ODI) — Validated Outcome Measure
Fear-Avoidance Beliefs Questionnaire (FABQ) — Validated Psychosocial Screen

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of total hip arthroplasty (CPT 27130) for our patient with unilateral primary osteoarthritis of the right hip (ICD-10 M16.11). The denial cited absent physical therapy. Updated documentation: (1) AP pelvis radiograph 2026-03-22 demonstrates Tönnis grade 3 superior joint-space obliteration with subchondral cyst formation and osteophytes; (2) supervised PT 14 visits across 12 weeks 2025-12-02 through 2026-02-25 documented (log attached); (3) pharmacologic ladder: acetaminophen, celecoxib 200 mg daily 10 weeks limited by ankle edema, duloxetine 60 mg daily 8 weeks; (4) intra-articular fluoroscopically-guided hip injection 2026-01-14 with 4-week relief, repeat injection 2026-04-08 with 2-week relief; (5) Harris Hip Score = 42 (poor); (6) functional limitation: cannot don socks, limited weight-bearing tolerance, sleep disruption from rest pain. Per Cigna Medical Coverage Policy 0515 Musculoskeletal Procedures and the AAOS Clinical Practice Guideline for the Management of Osteoarthritis of the Hip (2024), this patient meets all medical-necessity criteria — the 2024 AAOS update includes strong recommendations for PT and pharmacologic management prior to THA, both of which have been completed. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: imaging, PT log, medication record, injection records, Harris Hip Score.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy 0515: Musculoskeletal Procedures
AAOS Clinical Practice Guideline: Management of Osteoarthritis of the Hip (2024)
Harris Hip Score (HHS) — Validated Outcome Measure
American Physical Therapy Association (APTA): Management of Osteoarthritis of the Hip — CPG

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of lumbar discectomy (CPT 63030) for our patient with L5-S1 disc herniation and S1 radiculopathy (ICD-10 corrected to M51.17, intervertebral disc disorder with radiculopathy, lumbosacral region). The denial referenced isolated axial pain. The MRI report and clinical findings establish radicular pathology: (1) MRI 2026-04-01 reports a 7 mm right paracentral L5-S1 disc extrusion with S1 root impingement; (2) physical examination: positive straight-leg raise at 35° on right reproducing posterolateral leg pain to the lateral foot, decreased ankle jerk reflex right (0/2), 4/5 plantar-flexion weakness right; (3) EMG/NCS 2026-04-15 confirms acute S1 radiculopathy on the right; (4) failed 8 weeks of conservative management (PT, NSAID, gabapentin); (5) two L5-S1 selective nerve-root blocks with transient relief. Per Cigna/EviCore CMM-609 family musculoskeletal policies and the NASS Coverage Policy Recommendations for Lumbar Discectomy, all four criteria are met: MRI-confirmed herniation correlating with dermatomal symptoms, failed conservative management ≥6 weeks, motor and reflex deficits, and absence of isolated axial pain. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: MRI, neuro exam, EMG/NCS, PT log, injection records.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy / EviCore CMM-609: Lumbar Fusion (Arthrodesis) (V2.0.2025, Eff. December 18, 2025)
NASS Draft Model Coverage Policy: Lumbar Discectomy
NASS Coverage Policy Recommendations Hub (spine.org/Coverage)

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of autologous chondrocyte implantation (CPT 27412) for our patient with a discrete full-thickness chondral defect of the medial femoral condyle (ICD-10 updated to M93.261, osteochondritis dissecans of right knee). The denial cited absence of staged arthroscopy or lesion measurement. Subsequent staging: (1) Staged diagnostic arthroscopy 2026-03-29 confirms an ICRS grade IV cartilage defect measuring 3.2 cm² on the central weight-bearing portion of the medial femoral condyle, biopsy taken; (2) intact menisci confirmed at arthroscopy; (3) mechanical alignment radiographs demonstrate 2° varus (within acceptable range without osteotomy); (4) failed 6 months of conservative care including PT, NSAID, hyaluronic acid injection. Per Cigna Medical Coverage Policy 0515 Musculoskeletal Procedures, all four ACI criteria are satisfied. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: arthroscopy report, intra-operative photographs, lesion measurement, alignment radiographs, conservative-care log.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy 0515: Musculoskeletal Procedures
AAOS Clinical Practice Guideline: Management of Osteoarthritis of the Knee (Non-Arthroplasty), 3rd Edition (2021)

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of diagnostic knee arthroscopy (CPT 29870). The denial cited duplicative imaging. Updated documentation establishes diagnostic non-conclusiveness: (1) MRI 2026-04-02 was non-diagnostic for the patient's mechanical symptoms (sensitive to internal metallic artifact from a prior tibial-plateau ORIF, with non-evaluable medial joint compartment per the radiologist's report); (2) MRI arthrography 2026-04-22 also showed artifact precluding evaluation of meniscal structure; (3) the patient has persistent true locking with positive McMurray and effusion (documented 3 episodes over 6 weeks); (4) clinical pretest probability of internal derangement is high; (5) no Kellgren-Lawrence grade ≥3 OA was demonstrated on radiograph. Per Cigna/EviCore CMM-312, diagnostic arthroscopy is medically necessary when prior advanced imaging is non-diagnostic in the setting of mechanical symptoms; both MRI and MR arthrography are documented as non-diagnostic. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: both MRI reports, radiograph, exam documentation.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy / EviCore CMM-312: Knee Surgery — Arthroscopic and Open Procedures (Eff. March 7, 2026)

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of percutaneous SI joint fusion (CPT 27279). The denial cited single diagnostic block. Updated documentation: (1) Provocative SI exam with 4 of 5 positive maneuvers (Patrick's, Gaenslen's, distraction, thigh thrust positive; compression negative); (2) two fluoroscopically-guided diagnostic SI injections on separate dates 2026-02-14 and 2026-03-21 with documented ≥80% pain relief on each occasion (concordant dual-block); (3) failed 9 months of non-operative care including PT, sacroiliac belt, NSAID, two radiofrequency ablations of lateral branches with limited duration of relief; (4) MRI lumbar spine 2026-01-08 excludes co-existing lumbar pain generator at the symptomatic level; (5) ODI = 56 (severe). Per Cigna/EviCore CMM-609 family policy for SI fusion, all four criteria — provocative testing, dual diagnostic block with concordant relief, failed conservative care, exclusion of other generators — are satisfied. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: provocative exam form, both injection records, MRI, RFA records, PT log, ODI.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy / EviCore CMM-609: Lumbar Fusion (Arthrodesis) (V2.0.2025, Eff. December 18, 2025)
Oswestry Disability Index (ODI) — Validated Outcome Measure

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of combined posterior and posterolateral lumbar arthrodesis (CPT 22633) at L3-L4 for our patient with grade II lumbar spondylolisthesis (ICD-10 M43.16). Updated documentation: (1) standing flexion-extension lumbar radiographs 2026-04-12 demonstrate 14 mm anterolisthesis of L3 on L4 with 5 mm dynamic translation; (2) MRI 2026-04-12 demonstrates severe lateral recess stenosis with L4 root entrapment; (3) symptoms include bilateral L4 radicular pain and neurogenic claudication limiting walking to 75 meters; (4) supervised PT 18 visits over 16 weeks documented (log attached); (5) two lumbar epidural injections with transient relief; (6) Oswestry Disability Index = 70 (crippled), increased from baseline 52 over 6 months; (7) FABQ-physical = 19 (within reassuring range, supporting surgical candidacy without psychosocial contraindication). Per Cigna Medical Coverage Policy 0303 and NASS Coverage Policy Recommendations for Lumbar Fusion, fusion for grade II spondylolisthesis with neural compression after failed conservative care meets medical-necessity criteria. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: flexion-extension films, MRI, PT log, ESI records, ODI, FABQ.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy 0303: Lumbar Fusion for Spinal Instability and Degenerative Disc Conditions
NASS Coverage Recommendations: Lumbar Fusion (2021 update)
Oswestry Disability Index (ODI) — Validated Outcome Measure
Fear-Avoidance Beliefs Questionnaire (FABQ) — Validated Psychosocial Screen

appeal_text: We request expedited first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of revision total knee arthroplasty with allograft (CPT 27487) for our patient with prosthetic joint infection of the right knee (ICD-10 T84.53XA). The denial cited absent OON prior authorization. (1) The receiving facility is the only revision-arthroplasty center within the Cigna network gap area certified for two-stage exchange with allograft reconstruction — Cigna's published in-network revision center list does not include any facility within 75 miles of the patient's residence, satisfying network-inadequacy criteria; (2) the rendering surgeon is a board-certified orthopedic surgeon with fellowship training in adult reconstruction and a documented case-volume of >50 two-stage exchanges annually; (3) the patient presented with acute hematogenous infection requiring urgent operative debridement — synovial fluid leukocyte count 18,500/μL with 92% PMN, ESR 84, CRP 116, blood cultures positive for S. aureus; (4) emergent operative debridement and first-stage spacer placement was indicated within 48 hours. Per Cigna/EviCore CMM-311 revision criteria, revision for confirmed PJI is medically necessary; OON access in network gap with documented clinical urgency satisfies the OON exception criteria. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: network gap analysis, infectious disease consult, lab results, operative report, surgeon credentialing.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy / EviCore CMM-311: Knee Replacement Arthroplasty (V2.0.2025, Eff. March 7, 2026)

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of cervical arthrodesis (CPT 22551) at C5-C6 for our patient with cervical disc displacement and myelopathy (ICD-10 M50.022). Documentation: (1) MRI cervical spine 2026-04-18 demonstrates C5-C6 central disc herniation with cord compression and a focal high-T2 cord signal change consistent with myelomalacia; (2) physical examination: hyperreflexia in lower extremities (3+ patellar/Achilles), positive Hoffmann sign bilaterally, sustained ankle clonus, mJOA score = 11 (moderate cervical myelopathy), Nurick grade 3; (3) progressive symptoms over 4 months: hand-clumsiness and balance impairment; (4) failed 8 weeks of supervised PT with worsening myelopathic findings — PT was terminated by treating physiatrist due to risk of cord injury; (5) AOSpine recommendation for surgical management of moderate cervical myelopathy. Per Cigna/EviCore CMM-606 Cervical Spine Surgery and NASS coverage recommendations, surgical management of cervical myelopathy with cord signal change is a clear medical-necessity indication and conservative care extension is contraindicated. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: MRI, neuro exam, mJOA, PT progress notes.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy / EviCore CMM-606: Cervical Spine Surgery
NASS Coverage Policy Recommendations Hub (spine.org/Coverage)

appeal_text: We request first-level internal reconsideration of the denial of minimally invasive partial unicompartmental knee arthroplasty (CPT 27446) for our patient with isolated medial-compartment unilateral primary osteoarthritis of the left knee (ICD-10 M17.12). The denial cited that minimally invasive arthroplasty is not covered. Per Cigna Medical Coverage Policy 0347 Minimally Invasive Knee Replacement, unicompartmental and minimally invasive arthroplasty are medically necessary when (1) disease is confined to a single compartment radiographically and intraoperatively, (2) ligamentous integrity is preserved, (3) preoperative range of motion is adequate, and (4) the patient meets standard arthroplasty criteria. Documentation: (1) standing radiographs 2026-04-09 demonstrate Kellgren-Lawrence grade 4 medial-compartment disease with preserved lateral compartment (KL 1) and patellofemoral compartment (KL 1); (2) MRI 2026-04-11 confirms intact ACL and PCL; (3) preoperative ROM 0-130°; (4) full conservative-care documentation: 16 weeks PT, NSAID ladder, two intra-articular CSI; (5) Knee Society Score = 40, WOMAC = 76. AAOS CPG and AAHKS position statements support unicompartmental arthroplasty in well-selected patients. Submitted within the 180-day window. Attached: imaging, MRI, ROM, PT/injection log, KSS/WOMAC.
citations: Cigna Medical Coverage Policy 0347: Minimally Invasive Knee Replacement
AAOS Clinical Practice Guideline: Management of Osteoarthritis of the Knee (Non-Arthroplasty), 3rd Edition (2021)
American Association of Hip and Knee Surgeons (AAHKS): Position Statements
WOMAC Osteoarthritis Index — Validated Outcome Measure
